title: Hymenasplenium pubirhizoma
intro: Hymenasplenium pubirhizoma, also known as the hairy rhizome spleenwort, is a species of fern native to East Asia, characterized by its unique hairy rhizome and pinnate fronds. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of H. pubirhizoma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used in dried forms for capsules and tablets, and is also used in the form of extracts.
taxonomy: Hymenasplenium pubirhizoma is a species of fern in the family Osmundaceae. It is a type of osmundaceous fern, which is a group of ferns characterized by having a unique type of spore-bearing organ called a sporangium. The Osmundaceae family is further divided into several genera, with Hymenasplenium being one of them. This family of ferns is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The classification of Hymenasplenium pubirhizoma is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Family: Osmundaceae, Genus: Hymenasplenium, Species: H. pubirhizoma.
